skill.name: unocss
skill.description: Use UnoCSS guidance for configuration, utility rules, shortcuts, presets, transformers, and Vite or Nuxt integration.
skill.summary: Configure and use UnoCSS with focused guidance for rules, presets, shortcuts, and integrations.
skill.author: antfu

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, network, filesystem, and script patterns, but reviewed samples are Markdown documentation examples for UnoCSS configuration. No executable skill code, credential exfiltration, malicious network target, or prompt injection attempt was found, so the remaining concern is low documentation-example risk.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fence Command False Positives
low_findings.description: The external command findings are caused by Markdown code fences and inline examples, not by executable Ruby or shell code in the skill package.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Reviewed locations are documentation metadata, Markdown tables, or TypeScript examples. No executable script entry point or command invocation mechanism was found.

low_findings.title: Documentation Network Examples
low_findings.description: Hardcoded URLs and one fetch example appear in reference documentation to explain UnoCSS content extraction and source links.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed URL and fetch patterns are presented as user-facing examples or source references. I found no evidence that the skill itself performs network requests.

low_findings.title: Local Project Path Examples
low_findings.description: Filesystem findings are configuration examples for project-relative files, generated Nuxt config, or local font cache paths.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The path examples are common UnoCSS and Nuxt configuration patterns. They do not show arbitrary file reads, secret paths, or traversal used by skill code.

low_findings.title: Frontend Example Patterns
low_findings.description: The innerHTML and dynamic import findings are frontend integration snippets, not marketplace runtime behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed snippets are static examples for Shadow DOM placeholders and browser icon loading. They are not wired to user input inside the skill package.

content.value_statement: UnoCSS setup can be difficult when presets, shortcuts, variants, and integrations interact. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code focused reference guidance for practical UnoCSS work.
actual_capabilities: Explains UnoCSS config files and common defineConfig options.
Guides custom utility rules, dynamic rules, shortcuts, and safelists.
Covers Wind, Mini, Icons, Attributify, Typography, and Web Fonts presets.
Documents transformers for directives, variant groups, compiled classes, and JSX attributify syntax.
Provides Vite and Nuxt integration guidance with framework-specific notes.
limitations: Does not run UnoCSS or validate generated CSS output.
Does not replace the active project configuration as the source of truth.
May not cover UnoCSS features added after version 66.x.
Requires the agent to inspect local config files before using advanced syntax.

use_cases.title: Set Up UnoCSS
use_cases.description: Add a project config, choose presets, and wire UnoCSS into Vite or Nuxt.

use_cases.title: Design Utility APIs
use_cases.description: Create rules, shortcuts, safelists, and variants that match a design system.

use_cases.title: Migrate Tailwind Patterns
use_cases.description: Map familiar Tailwind CSS utility workflows to UnoCSS presets and transformers.

prompt_templates.title: Create Basic Config
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me create a basic UnoCSS config for this project. Inspect existing config files first, then suggest safe presets and imports.

prompt_templates.title: Add Shortcuts
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my repeated utility classes and propose UnoCSS shortcuts. Keep names clear and compatible with the current preset setup.

prompt_templates.title: Build Custom Rules
prompt_templates.prompt: Design UnoCSS static and dynamic rules for these design tokens. Explain matching, generated CSS behavior, and edge cases.

prompt_templates.title: Tune Advanced Integration
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit my UnoCSS Vite or Nuxt integration. Check presets, transformers, extraction, safelist, layers, and performance tradeoffs.

output_examples.input: A Vue app needs UnoCSS with icons, typography, and variant groups.
output_examples.output: A setup plan listing required packages, config sections, plugin registration, and checks for existing UnoCSS config files.

output_examples.input: A team repeats button and badge utility strings across components.
output_examples.output: A shortcut design proposal with naming guidance, token alignment, and notes about when dynamic shortcuts are appropriate.

output_examples.input: A Nuxt project has generated UnoCSS settings and local overrides.
output_examples.output: A review summary explaining how to extend merged config safely and where to keep project-specific shortcuts.

best_practices: Inspect uno.config.* or unocss.config.* before recommending advanced syntax.
Prefer explicit class usage unless the project already enables attributify or tagify modes.
Keep custom rules and shortcuts aligned with existing theme tokens.
anti_patterns: Do not assume every Tailwind plugin or class works without checking the active preset.
Do not add attributify, tagify, or transformers without confirming project support.
Do not create broad dynamic rules when a small static rule or shortcut is clearer.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps agents configure UnoCSS, write utilities, use presets, and integrate UnoCSS with Vite or Nuxt.

faq.question: Does it support Tailwind CSS users?
faq.answer: Yes. UnoCSS can use Tailwind-compatible presets, so the skill explains familiar utility patterns in UnoCSS terms.

faq.question: Can it generate a full design system?
faq.answer: It can help structure rules, shortcuts, and theme tokens, but the project design requirements must come from the user.

faq.question: Does it execute UnoCSS commands?
faq.answer: No. The skill provides reference guidance and examples. It does not run commands by itself.

faq.question: Should agents inspect local files first?
faq.answer: Yes. Existing UnoCSS config files define available presets, rules, shortcuts, and transformers.

faq.question: Which integrations are covered?
faq.answer: The references cover Vite and Nuxt integration, plus notes for common framework usage.
